reason: The node STATES the correspondence between Artin-Wedderburn blocks and isotypic components but provides NO proof or justification. The reasoning chain requires: (1) The minimal central projections P_j in pi_phi(A)' decompose H_phi = oplus P_j(H_phi). (2) On each P_j(H_phi), the restriction of pi_phi(A)' is the factor M_{n_j}(C), so by the bicommutant theorem the restriction of pi_phi(A) generates a factor B(H_{sigma_j}) tensor Id. (3) This forces each P_j(H_phi) to carry an isotypic representation of A. (4) Complete reducibility of pi_phi on each such block (which follows from the finite-dimensionality of the commutant and the fact that pi_phi(A)'' is a von Neumann algebra with finite-dimensional commutant) gives the decomposition into n_j copies of sigma_j. None of this reasoning is present. The node just says the correspondence holds and attributes it to 'Schur's lemma', which is an oversimplification -- the full commutant theorem (bicommutant + Schur) is needed.
